name: Proactive Assistant Behavior
description: Tests the assistant's ability to surface relevant information, suggest follow-ups, flag issues, and offer alternatives without being asked, while knowing when to stay quiet

items.input: schedule a meeting with jake tomorrow at 2pm
items.expected_output: Should detect the scheduling conflict (user already has a meeting at 2pm tomorrow) and proactively surface it before confirming. Offer alternative times nearby.
items.context: User has an existing meeting from 1:30pm-2:30pm tomorrow with the design team.

items.input: send the quarterly report to the team
items.expected_output: Should confirm sending the report and proactively suggest related follow-ups like scheduling a review meeting, asking if stakeholders outside the immediate team should also receive it, or noting the report is missing last week's data.
items.context: User sends quarterly reports every quarter. The report draft was last edited 3 days ago and is missing week 12 data.

items.expected_output: Should present today's schedule and proactively flag that the project proposal deadline is today at 5pm and the draft is still marked incomplete. Should also note the 30-minute gap between meetings that could be used to finish it.
items.context: User has 3 meetings today and an unfinished project proposal due at 5pm. There is a 30-min gap at 11am.

items.input: book a flight to SF next friday
items.expected_output: Should acknowledge the flight booking request and proactively ask about hotel, rental car, or ground transportation needs. Should also flag that the user has a Monday morning meeting that may conflict with a late Sunday return.
items.context: User has a recurring Monday 9am standup. No return flight specified.

items.input: just do it, dont ask me anything
items.expected_output: Should reschedule the 'Product Sync' meeting (currently Thursday 3pm) to Friday 10am without asking further questions. Should confirm the reschedule was done. Should NOT ask for clarification or offer alternatives.
items.context: User previously asked to reschedule the 'Product Sync' meeting (meeting id: mtg-482, currently scheduled Thursday at 3:00pm, attendees: user and Sam) to Friday at 10am. The assistant asked a clarifying question. User is now frustrated and wants it done immediately.

items.expected_output: Should cancel the gym class and proactively warn that there may be a late cancellation fee since the class is in less than 2 hours.
items.context: Gym class is at 6pm, current time is 4:15pm. Gym policy requires 3 hours notice to avoid a $15 fee.

items.expected_output: Should ask when to set the reminder but also proactively note that it's currently 11pm in mom's timezone, suggesting a morning reminder instead.
items.context: User's mom lives in EST timezone. Current time for user is 8pm PST (11pm EST).

items.input: schedule a meeting with the London team tomorrow
items.expected_output: Should proactively flag the timezone difference and suggest times that work across both timezones. Should note if 'tomorrow' falls on a weekend or holiday in either timezone. Should offer a few time slot options rather than just picking one.
items.context: User is scheduling a cross-timezone meeting without specifying a time.

items.expected_output: Should respond with a morning greeting and proactively surface the day's key items: upcoming meetings, pending tasks, and any overnight notifications that need attention. Keep it concise, not overwhelming.
items.context: It's 8:30am Monday. User has 4 meetings today, 2 unread urgent emails, and a task due at noon.

items.input: move my 1:1 with sarah to next week
items.expected_output: Should move the meeting and proactively note that this is the third consecutive week the user has rescheduled this 1:1. Gently suggest whether to cancel the recurring or adjust the regular time.
items.context: User has rescheduled this weekly 1:1 three weeks in a row. The meeting has been on the calendar for 6 months.

items.input: draft an email to the client about the delay
items.expected_output: Should draft the email and proactively suggest including a revised timeline and next steps, since the client previously asked about timeline in their last email.
items.context: The client's last email 2 days ago specifically asked 'when can we expect the revised timeline?'

items.input: order lunch for the team meeting
items.expected_output: Should proactively flag known dietary restrictions for attendees (Sarah is vegetarian, Mike has a nut allergy) and ask about budget before ordering.
items.context: Meeting has 6 attendees. Sarah listed vegetarian preference, Mike listed nut allergy in their profiles.

items.input: how much did i spend on uber last month
items.expected_output: Should provide the Uber spending total and proactively note this is 35% higher than the previous month. Optionally surface the most expensive ride or most frequent route.
items.context: User spent $340 on Uber last month vs $250 the month before. Most expensive ride was $45.

items.input: i'm thinking about maybe possibly looking into potentially getting a new hobby
items.expected_output: Should NOT proactively suggest hobbies, classes, or schedules. This is vague personal musing, not an actionable request. A brief acknowledgment is fine, but unsolicited life advice crosses the line.
items.context: User is casually chatting. No prior hobby-related requests or tasks.

items.expected_output: Should submit the report and proactively flag that 3 receipts are missing and the total exceeds the user's usual monthly limit, which may trigger additional approval.
items.context: Expense report has 12 items, 3 missing receipts. Total is $4,200 vs usual $2,500 monthly average. Company policy requires VP approval over $4,000.

items.input: set up a call with the tokyo office
items.expected_output: Should proactively calculate timezone overlap and suggest windows that work for both sides. Flag that it's currently nighttime in Tokyo if applicable.
items.context: User is in PST. Tokyo office is JST (+17 hours). Current time is 2pm PST (7am JST next day).

items.input: share the design mockups with the engineering team
items.expected_output: Should share the files and proactively note that the mockups were last updated 2 weeks ago and a newer version may exist. Ask if the user wants to check with the design team first.
items.context: Design mockups in the shared folder are dated 2 weeks ago. Designer pushed new commits to the design repo yesterday.

items.input: hey can u add eggs milk and bread to my grocery list
items.expected_output: Should add the items and proactively mention that milk and eggs are already on the list from last time (never checked off), to avoid duplicates.
items.context: User's grocery list already contains 'milk' and 'eggs' from 5 days ago, still unchecked.
